sn35.146:1.1: “Monks, I will teach you new & old kamma, the cessation of kamma, and the path of practice leading to the cessation of kamma.
sn35.146:1.2: Listen & pay close attention. I will speak.
sn35.146:1.4: The eye is to be seen as old kamma, fabricated & willed, capable of being felt.
sn35.146:1.5: The ear… The nose…The tongue… The body…
sn35.146:1.6: The intellect is to be seen as old kamma, fabricated & willed, capable of being felt.
sn35.146:1.9: Whatever kamma one does now with the body, with speech, or with the intellect:
sn35.146:1.11: “And what is the cessation of kamma?
sn35.146:1.12: Whoever touches the release that comes from the cessation of bodily kamma, verbal kamma, & mental kamma:
sn35.146:1.13: This is called the cessation of kamma.
sn35.146:1.14: “And what is the path of practice leading to the cessation of kamma?
sn35.146:1.16: right view, right resolve, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, right concentration.
sn35.146:1.17: This is called the path of practice leading to the cessation of kamma.
sn35.146:1.18: “So, monks, I have taught you new & old kamma, the cessation of kamma, and the path of practice leading to the cessation of kamma.
sn35.146:1.19: Whatever a teacher should do—seeking the welfare of his disciples, out of sympathy for them—that have I done for you.
sn35.146:1.20: Over there are the roots of trees; over there, empty dwellings. Practice jhāna, monks. Don’t be heedless. Don’t later fall into regret. This is our message to you.”
